title: agropyron repens subsp. pseudocaesium
intro: Agropyron repens subsp. pseudocaesium, also known as creeping bentgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the wheatgrass family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Agropyron repens subsp. pseudocaesium can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
